learning_outcomes.description: Discern and evaluate existing and emerging system models that underpin approaches to dealing with risk and safe operation in complex industrial systems. Develop Acci-Map representations of system failures
Analyse complex industrial systems to determine best actions in design to address risks through inherently safer design principles
Critique and perform layer of protection analysis (LOPA) studies so as to assess risk levels and risk reduction strategies related to process plant
Design and specify safety instrumented systems (SIS) for specific industrial case studies along with their implementation and assurance
Evaluate the interaction of humans within complex engineered systems in order to enhance system resilience
Investigate, analyse and design strategies for operator actions in industrial applications using cognitive work analysis (CWA) and strategy development and assessment
Communicate assignment and project outcomes clearly, concisely and persuasively, in both written and oral formats, appropriate to the target audience
Improve team work and communication skills through practice on projects

assessments.task_description:
Group Assignment.

More detailed guidance will be given in class.

Submissions will be through Blackboard. A Turnitin link will be made available before the due date.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: PDF must be submitted through Blackboard through the link provided.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Group project.

Industry based project dealing with course content and skills applied to actual process design and/or operational issue. 

Submissions will be through Blackboard. A Turnitin link will be made available before the due date, as well as a submission portal for prepared questions for the site visit.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submissions will be through Blackboard. A Turnitin link will be made available before the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Exam During Exam Period (School) - Oral Examination
assessments.task_description:
This will consist of an interview session which will be held during the exam period. 20 minutes will be allocated to each student.

All course content and case studies are assessable during the oral exam. The exam timetable will be made available on Blackboard.

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials

There are two-hour tutorial sessions each week that will provide students with theory as well as opportunities to discuss and practically apply the theory and techniques taught. These are compulsory for all students to attend. The attendance and participation in these session will be in part reflected in the moderated peer assessment.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L08
